sourceMovie.title: Seed of Chucky
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Seed of Chucky for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Don Mancini keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Haunted House 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Seed of Chucky clicked for you, A Haunted House 2 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Seed of Chucky?
faq.answer: Try A Haunted House 2, The Witches of Eastwick, Halloween—each shares DNA with Seed of Chucky while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Seed of Chucky?
faq.answer: Seed of Chucky (2004) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Seed of Chucky part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Child's Play Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
